summaries.content: This lesson introduces the excitement of travelling to visit grandparents. Whether grandparents live in a village, a town, or another city, the journey to their house is always an adventure. Students learn about different modes of travel - trains, buses, cars, boats, and even bullock carts in some villages. The lesson highlights that the way we travel depends on the distance, the geography of the area, and the transport available.
keyPoints: Travelling to grandparents' house is an exciting experience
Different families use different modes of transport
The way we travel depends on distance and geography
Every journey is an opportunity to observe and learn

summaries.content: This lesson explores family relationships and the different members of a family. Students learn the names for relatives on both the maternal (mother's) and paternal (father's) side. In Hindi, there are specific words for each relationship - Nana-Nani for maternal grandparents, Dada-Dadi for paternal grandparents, Mama for mother's brother, Chacha for father's brother, and so on. The lesson helps students understand how a family tree works and how everyone is connected.
keyPoints: Every family member has a specific relationship name
Maternal means mother's side and paternal means father's side
Hindi has unique words for each family relationship
A family tree shows how all family members are connected

summaries.content: This lesson focuses on what makes grandmother's house special. Students share their experiences of visiting grandparents - the special food, the stories, the games, and the love. Grandparents often live differently from parents, especially if they live in a village. The lesson shows how life in a village differs from city life - open spaces, farm animals, different food, and a slower pace. Students compare their home with their grandparents' home and note the differences.
keyPoints: Grandparents' houses have special memories and traditions
Village life is different from city life in many ways
Grandparents share stories, recipes, and traditions
Comparing two homes helps us understand different lifestyles

summaries.content: This lesson emphasises the importance of family bonds and learning from elders. Grandparents and other elders carry the history and traditions of the family. They can tell us about how life was different when they were young - what games they played, what they ate, how they studied, and how the world has changed. The lesson encourages students to talk to their elders, ask questions, and record family stories before they are forgotten. Every family has a rich history waiting to be discovered.
keyPoints: Elders carry family history and traditions
Life was very different when grandparents were young
Talking to elders helps us learn and stay connected
Every family has unique stories worth preserving
